background : bipolar hip arthroplasty ( bha ) is the method of choice for the treatment of advanced avascular necrosis ( avn ) of the femoral head . however , its overuse may lead to increased wear and need for early revision.aims:this retrospective series has assessed the midterm outcome of bha in young adults ( age < 60 ) with ficat stages 3 and 4 avn.materials and methods : eighty consecutive patients below 60 years of age ( 96 hips ) were operated by the senior author between 1995 and 2010 using bha with tight fitting cup technique . all patients were diagnosed as idiopathic avn , 8 were posttraumatic avn and 12 were post alcohol consumption avn .  harrowing a subgroup analysis was also done , by dividing the cohort according to ficats 3 , 4 and 5 subgroups.results:all patients were found to have mild to moderate acetabular involvement as per steinberg et al . . the mean age of the patients was 42  6.44 years ( range 30 - 59 years ) . the median age of all patients was 45 years . all the patients were called for a final followup for clinical and radiological assessment.conclusion:this procedure can be

valproic acid ( vpa ) is a short - branched chain fatty acid which has been used in the treatment of various neurological disorders . the present case report is about a 25-year - old hispanic female who was admitted to the intensive care unit after attempted suicide by consuming an unknown number of vpa pills . the patient 's vpa levels were elevated with concentrations known to be toxic and that toxic effects observed were more severe with high dose and less severe when secondary hyperammonemia decreased . the serum ammonia normalized log - linear plot showed a down - trending linear first - order kinetics with a calculated t = 15.08 h . the normalization for each compound showed two different decay kinetics which are not related to hepatic damage or carnitine deficiency .  we also made the following observations : ( 1 ) raised venous ammonia levels may remain symptomatic for a significant period of time beyond discontinuation of the drug .  ( 2 ) existence of more than one concurrent mechanism with different decay ketics in the metabolism of ammonia in presence of high or low concentrations of serum vpa .

intramammary infection ( mastitis ) is the most common reason for the use of antimicrobials in dairy cows .  the aim of this article is to review current treatments of mastitis during lactation and seek for evidence - based , best practice treatment recommendations for bovine mastitis .

human immunodeficiency virus ( hiv ) is a vector - borne virus , and transmission of hiv to humans is extremely rare . this article addresses the question of why hiv lacks vector transmission , both through a consideration of available empirical data and through the construction of mathematical models . we argue that , in principle , hiv is capable of vector transmission because of a lack of appropriate genetic variation . however , we also argue that this lack of transmission is due to the fact that the virus is unable to replicate within arthropod vectors , and that this is a function of the level of viremia in humans , rather than of the nature and strength of selection for this route of transmission . we conclude that , despite evidence that mechanical transmission of human hiv by arthropods is possible , there is no evidence to support the spread of biological transmission in hiv or other retroviruses . we propose that , by asking such questions , we can gain deeper insight into the evolution of human human immunodermal transmission .

background : in the normal kidney , immunohistochemical expression of contractile protein alpha - smooth muscle actin ( asma ) is limited to the vascular smooth muscle cells . in pathological conditions , the expression of asma in interstitium is related to damaged renal function and progression of disease . the aim of this study was to analyze the correlation of aspa expression in glomerulonephritis and renal function at the time of biopsy.materials and methods : this retrospective study analyzed 142 patients  82 adults and 60 children  who underwent percutaneous renal biopsy in the department of internal medicine and department of pediatrics , university hospital , split , croatia in the period from 1994 to 2007.results:in the proliferative category , the mean absolute values of blood pressure ( bp ) , serum creatinine clearance ( ccr ) and 24-hour urine protein were measured within 7 days before the biopsy . in the non - proliferative categories , the average absolute values were 0.058 and 0.0009 , respectively . in adults , the significance was confirmed by the correlation between asma expression and ccr ( r=0.45 , p<0.002 ) . in children , absolute values ( sc )

chondroblastoma accounts for < 1% of all primary bone tumors . usually occurring in young adolescents ,  we report cytomorphology in two cases of chondrobloma confirmed by histopathology with a brief review of literature .  we also report the cytomorphological features of chicken wire calcification in the two cases .

objective . to investigate whether parental bonding is associated with attitudes toward suicide among medical college students in japan.methods . 160 questionnaires ( 116 males and 44 females ) were distributed to 226 medical students . the parental bonding instrument ( pbi )  Scythe was administered to each participant to measure the attitudes and behavior of the parents.results .  Scythe higher scores on the care and protection dimensions reveal that participants perceive their parents to be more caring and/or protective.conclusion . although parental bonding predisposes these attitudes toward suicidal behavior , educational programs could change caretaking practices .

backgroundthe aim of this study is to investigate the adherence to gold 2010 guideline for the treatment of chronic obstructive lung disease ( copd ) among pulmonary physicians in turkey.methodsthe study was carried out in eleven pulmonary outpatient clinics of eleven cities ( adana , ankara , diyarbakir , erzurum , istanbul , izmir , kocaeli , and samsun ) across turkey.resultsthe median age of all the patients was 62.99.7 years . the most commonly used bronchodilator was laba ( 43.4% ) , followed by laba - ics - lama - t ( 22.5% ) and lama ( 27.2% ) . in severe and very severe disease stages , the most frequently used treatment options were long - acting muscarinic antagonist ( lama ) ( 47.1% ) or combination of bronchiectasis ( ics ) . the appropriate treatment rate was 83.4%.conclusionsthe guideline guidelines for copd in turkey are not applicable to all patients , and there is a need to improve adherence to the guideline guidelines .

backgroundamphotericin b ( amb ) , an amphoteric polyene macrolide , has been considered one of the main options of antifungal therapy for serious and life - threatening mycotic infections for over 50 years . however , different aspects of amb nephrotoxicity have not been studied well or in detail in our population.objectivesthe purpose of this study was to specifically assess the frequency , time onset , and possible associated factors of amb - induced hypokalemia in hospitalized patients in hematology - oncology wards in the southwest of iran.patients and methodsduring the 9-month study period , 90 patients who were scheduled to receive amb were screened . the mean  sd daily oral / intravenous sodium supplementation was 124.75  62.15 meq , and the duration of liposomal amb infusion ( p = 0.141 ) was calculated . the univariate logistic regression analysis with the odds ratio ( or ) and 95% confidence interval ( ci ) was employed to select all possible variables linked to the dependent variable.resultsthe incidence ( 27.5% ) and time onset ( 5.06  3.35 days ) were comparable between the patients with and without amb ( 54.

objective : the purpose of this study was to determine the main o - glycan structures in snails and characterize a common core - trisaccharide . materials and methods : the o - glycans of a. lusitanicus , cepaea hortensis , planorbarius corneus and helix pomatia were collected by the authors under the supervision of dr . regor manfred pintar ( department of integrative biology and biodiversity research , institute of zoology , university of natural resources and life sciences , vienna ) in the vicinity of vienna.results:the main structures of snails were identified by gc - esi - ms and by electron impact ( ei ) analysis of the corresponding alditol acetates . the most common o-glycan structures were those of n- glycans , which were elongated by one or two unmethylated hexoses . the three most abundant types of o -glycan were galnac and galnacetose . the four most frequent types of hexoses were h - reduced , galnace and glcnac , while all the other sugars were h reduced . the fourth type was also h reduced , while the fifth type was galnaco . the

background : adiponectin ( arcp 30 , adipoq , apm1 or gbp28 ) , secreted by adipose tissue , is a 247-amino acid peptide which was discovered in 1995 . the amounts of adiponectionin account for 0.01% of total plasma protein .  the aim of this study was to determine the association between adiponctin and coronary artery disease.methods:the study population consisted of 82 consecutive patients who underwent coronary angiography for suspected or known coronary atherosclerosis at the university clinical center of kosovo . the patients were divided into two groups : cad group ( mean age 66.76 + 9.12 ) and non - cad group . the cad group patients were older and were more likely to be male and smokers.results:the mean gensini score was 0.272 and the other significant variables were : diastolic blood pressure ( dbp ) , whr , lvef , smoking , and smoking.conclusion:the present study found that there was a strong association between fat distribution and coronary disease.conclusions:the findings provide further evidence for an  adiponectorin paradox  in which higher levels of fat are secreted as a compensatory

neurofibromatosis type - i ( nf-1 ) is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by disordered growth of ectodermal tissues , and is part of a group of disorders called phakomatoses ( neurocutaneous syndrome ) .  the localized form of neurofibroma type i , first described by gammel in 1931 , is very rare .  it is characterized by spots of increased skin pigmentation ( caf au lait spots ) , combined with peripheral nerve tumors and a variety of other dysplastic abnormalities of the skin , nervous system , bones , endocrine organs , and blood vessels .  we report a case of a 26-year - old female patient who presented to the dental department complaining about the unesthetic appearance of her face , since eight years .  she had a swelling on the face and the skin over the right side of her neck was thickened and hyperpigmented , with a soft and loose hanging overgrowth .

the zygomaticomaxillary complex is an important functional and aesthetic landmark of the midface , and it provides prominent cheek structure .  the zygoma is a well - known and well - recognized functional landmark . however , unfortunately it is very vulnerable to injury because of its intrinsically prominent convexity .  we report a case of a 78 yr - old patient who developed persistent ectropion when canthal reattachment had not been performed .  he was treated with a single transconjunctival incision ( frontozygomatic suture and inferior orbital rim ) .  his follow - up radiographs were routinely used to evaluate the adequacy of reduction .

background : gram - negative coccobacillus pasteurella multocida is a gram - positive coccanobacterium that is commonly found in the oral cavity of dogs and cats . it is a recognised cause of wound infection as a result of bites , licking or scratching . we herein report a case in which a 38-year - old man developed endocarditis due to the presence of a vegetation ( 0.57 0.2 cm ) above the atrium of the right coronary artery . the patient was treated with antibiotics and valve replacement was mandatory .

key clinical messagethe case presented in the report illustrates diagnostic and therapeutic difficulties that a urologist might encounter during treatment of patients with possible renal tumor . the patient underwent transperitoneal radical nephrectomy and the tumor was successfully removed through an incision below the costal margin .

introductions of populations at low density and/or small size are often faced with inverse density dependent effects , attributed to demographic stochasticity or reduced cooperative interactions . in this paper , we explore the feasibility of small populations that may adaptively respond to overcome these effects in order to establish , given any amount of genetic variation , an introduced population . we introduce a deterministic model of population dynamics with density - mediated growth ( i.e. , an allee effect ) and diffusive dispersal . we model the growth and spread of this population according to a reaction - diffusion equation , and allow evolution to influence the rate of change of the allee threshold . we show that the rate at which the population density changes is proportional to the fraction of genetic variance that is required for an invasion to succeed . we also show that evolution can influence the rates of population density and genetic variance .

background : chlamydia trachomatis infection ( cti ) is one of the most prevalent bacterial sexually transmitted infections ( stis ) in the developing world . the primary objective of this study was to establish the need for screening hiv seropositive and seronegative women for cti.methods:in this cross - sectional study , thirty adult hiv - infected women with symptoms suggestive of rtis ( study group a1 ) and thirty hiv- uninfected women without symptoms of cti ( control group b1 ) were recruited .  the mean age of the hiv positive subjects in our study was 30.92  5.7 and that of the control group was 28.52  6.9 .  all the study subjects were recruited as follows : thirty adult female seropoitive women with hiv symptoms ( study groups a1 , b2 , c2 , and c2 ) ,thirty hiv women with ct symptoms ( hiv ) , and thirty women without ct and hiv negative symptoms ( control groups b2 and c3 ) .  results : hiv infection was detected in 6.67% ( 4/60 ) hiv infected women and in 1.67 (

background and objectives :  the aim of this study was to test the hypothesis that fatal outcome following hip fracture erythematosus is associated with increased plasma troponin levels.methods:a total of 302 consecutive patients over 75  years of age with dislocated hip fractures were enrolled in the study at elverum ( ech ) and buskerud ( bch ) central hospitals during the period 20052009 .  the plasma levels of myocardium - specific creatine kinase ( tnt ) , creatine kinases ( ck ) , myocardial - specific creatinine kinase and ratio of ck - mb to ck were measured by electrochemiluminescence immunoassay ( elica , roche , basel , switzerland and abbott , abbott park , il , usa).results:in the mortality group , 59 of 302 ( 19.5% ) patients died . on the fourth postoperative day , tnt plasma levels rose nearly threefold ( inverse for the ratio ) . on days 1 and + 1 , ck and ck- mb values were significantly higher among those who died compared to those who survived ( p  =  0.001 and p =

background : acute retinal necrosis ( arn ) is one of the clinical presentations of herpes simplex virus ( hsv ) . it may present several years after a primary infection , or it may occur following systemic herpetic infection such as herpetics dermatitis . we report one case of unilateral arn 1 month after the herpanitis . the diagnosis was made by polymerase chain reaction of aqueous humor and the patient received intravenous acyclovir .

the rhomboid family is a family of intramembrane serine proteases , which is thought to have arisen in the last common ancestor of all extant life forms . the rhomboids have been characterized experimentally using several independent phylogenetic methods and the corresponding statistical tests .  the phylogenetic tree of this family was constructed using the least - squares method and the kishino - hasegawa test . the results were compared to the best phylogenetic trees constructed using a variety of independent methods for phylogenetic analysis and statistical analysis . the most recent results support the alternative hypothesis of monophyletic origin of eukaryotic rho and parl subfamilies , which postulates acquisition of a gene from the pro - mitochondrial endosymbiont .  furthermore , the phylogeny of this superfamily is supported by features of domain architectures that appear to comprise shared derived characters .

the infliction in the life expectancy figure of patients with sickle cell disease ( scd ) occurred around the middle of the 1970s . this minimal increase in life expectancy coincided with my appointment at thomas jefferson university as the associate director of the newly created adult sickle cells center . the net effect of an opioid depends on the availability of enzyme(s ) to convert it into metabolites that could be active or inactive .  The infliction point was around the time that the steady stream of admissions to the emergency department ( ed ) and hospital were not welcome by most providers , hospital administration , house and nursing staffs . the fact that most patients were barely educated , many without a high school degree , unemployed , mediocre health coverage , and dysfunctional family structure conferred a logarithmic dimension to the problem . soon labels such as drug addicts , drug - seeking behavior , and hospital hopping and frequent flyer emerged .  I believe this approach in diagnostics and therapeutics ushers in the dawn of a new field for the management of individual patients based on their unique pharmacogenetics , phenotypic and biomarker characteristics .

the potential of rnai to silence any gene has made it an attractive therapeutic modality .  the main obstacle to rnai in the clinic is delivery . to be effective , however , sirnas must be transported through the body , bind , and be taken up by target cells where they must traverse the plasma membrane and gain access to the cytosolic compartment , where the rnai machinery resides . to overcome this obstacle , lnps containing sirnas are designed to knock down gene silencing . we recently demonstrated that these lnp - coated lns can be used to selectively knock down specific genes in dcs .  we demonstrate that our approach enhances the efficacy of sirna - silencing in immune cells using lnp formulations .

sevoflurane is a potent anti - arrhythmogenic agent and has been shown to be safe in the treatment of hypertension . however , adverse cardiac arrhythmia such as nodal rhythm with bradycardia , prolongation of qtc interval , precipitation of torsade de pointes and isorhythmic atrioventricular dissociation ( isravd ) have been reported with sevofluurane anaesthesia . this paper presents a case of a woman who was judged to lack mental capacity to refuse life - saving treatment , and subsequently died . this paper argues that taking a diachronic perspective in assessments of mental capacity allows for decision - making to be compromised in the context of mental disorder . this perspective is supported by the view that the person 's commitment to the future is not contingent on their ability to understand , retain , and weigh what the likely costs and benefits of treatment will be for them , currently compromised . it also argues that this perspective makes it the case that the patient 's commitments to future personal consequences of choices are not contingent upon the person having mental capacity . it seems to me that this approach undermines the protections of patient autonomy conferred by making an individual assessment of functional capacities , rather than diagnosis , the proper grounds for infringements on self - determination .

type iii dysbetalipoproteinaemia is a kind of lipid metabolism disorders , caused by apo - e deficiency , which leads to accumulation of chylomicrons and very low density lipoprotein remnants in the plasma . xanthomatosis is usually associated with hyperlipidaemia , and morbidity and mortality of this condition are related to atherosclerosis and pancreatitis . we present a case of cauliflower xanthoma in a 43-year - old man .

key clinical messagethe most common benign tumor encountered in fatty bodies of the abdominal cavity is liposarcoma . lipoblastomatosis is a benign tumor characterized by aggressive localized growth . it can be found in the extremities and torso whereas abdominal cavity belongs to its rather rare sites . in us , such tumors are echogenic .  heifer lipobloma is a rare benign tumor , mainly composed of brown fat , in whose cells the numbers of mitochondria responsible for enhanced metabolic activity are impressive . it is usually found at the supra- and subhepatic areas . it has a characteristic feature of flow in color doppler . it occurs in the abdominal region . it also has a tendency to compress adjacent tissues or organs and become heterogeneous . in such cases , it is difficult to distinguish it from liposarccoma , which is a neoplasm . it should be noted that , in the presence of a large tumor , the lymph nodes , together with the superior mesenteric vessels , create a so - called sandwich  image .

background : thyroid fine needle aspiration ( fna ) cytology has become an international standard of thyroid cytology . in 2013 , the japan thyroid association ( jta ) published guidelines for clinical practice for the management of thyroid nodules , including its diagnostic system for reporting thyroid fna cytology as shown in table 1 . the jta guidelines recommended risk stratification of follicular neoplasms ( fn ) into benign ( low risk : lr ) , borderline ( moderate risk : mr ) and malignant ( high risk : hr ) categories.objectives:the aim of this study was to evaluate observer variation in the subclassification of fn among 4 thyroid experts to validate the usefulness and limitations of this characteristic risk stratifying of fn.patients and methods : the 4 reviewers ( kennichi kakudo , kaori kameyama , mitsuyoshi hirokawa , and ryohei katoh ) reviewed the cytological samples of patients with indeterminate thyroid nodule under indeterminates cytological diagnoses.results:the incidence of malignancy of fn was 31.3% ( 15/48 ) and that of lr was 16.7% ( 4/24 ) ( p = 0.002 ) . the

objective . to identify barriers to the diagnosis and treatment of acquired hemophilia and to identify diagnostic and therapeutic strategies for this potentially life - threatening condition . methods . a case - based survey was conducted of physicians in a number of specialties to determine barriers to their knowledge and practice . results . in total , 302 physicians ( 5051 per specialty ) participated in the survey .  hematologists and hematologist / oncologists accounted for the highest percentage ( 57% ) , followed by internal medicine and critical care specialists ( 47% and 47% , respectively ) . the most common reasons for consultation were  an unexplained abnormal lab or bleeding ( 33 like or similar mentions ) ( 47 ) ,  any unexplained bleeding disorder ( 33 ) , and  any other unexplained bleeding condition ( 33 ix , xi , or xii ) .  the majority of physicians ( 39% ) would have recommended hospital admission in response to the initial laboratory results , while the majority would have preferred hospital admission . conclusions . the findings of this survey suggest that physicians must harbor a high index of suspicion to diagnose this condition promptly in patients who present with recent - onset or acute bleeding .

despite recent advances in understanding of the mechanisms regulating stress responses and morphogenesis , our knowledge regarding the processes maintaining cell wall integrity ( cwi ) is still limited . in the last years , several reviews have been published on the biological role and function of cwi maintenance during biotic stress responses , illustrating the increased interest in this area . in this review , we will focus on recent developments regarding the role of the cwi in the perception of and response to biotic stresses .  we will also discuss the potential role of specific cell wall defects in understanding the response to stress responses .

background : the methylation statuses at selected locus of dna methylation in acute and chronic myeloid leukemia ( aml ) are highly methylated in bone marrow . however , the differential mechanisms through which these genes produce a different type of leukemia are not fully understood . in this study , we investigated the epigenetic modification of several methylation - controlled genes in aml and cml patients using a methylation specific polymerase chain reaction ( msp ) method.methods:a total of 23 bone marrow samples were obtained from 23 patients with aml , 21 patients with chronic myl , and 22 normal peripheral bloods were obtained . the msp results were analyzed as a dichotomous variable based on the presence or absence of gene methylation.results:the methylation frequencies of the dab2ip , dlc-1 , and runx3 genes were higher in acute leukemia ( p=0.001 , p<0.0001 , and p=1.001 ) and cl ( p<1.0001 ) patients compared to cml ( p =0.007 , p=2.006 ) . in addition , the frequencies of promoter hypermethylation of shp1 gene were significantly higher in cml compared to aml

background : vertical axis rotation following patellar dislocation is a rare variant of this type of injury and can prevent closed reduction in the acute setting . however , when the patella itself rotates around its vertical axis during dislocation , surgical intervention is usually required . the aim of this article is to describe a case of an irreducible patellal dislocation following vertical axis rot . methods : we describe a 32-year old gentleman who presented with a painful , immobile right knee . following unsuccessful attempts at closed reduction under sedation and regional nerve block , eventual open reduction and soft tissue reconstruction was required under general anesthetic . during the open reduction procedure , it was noted that the dislocation had dislocated into a lateral extra - articular position and rotated around the vertical axis .  harrowing a review of the literature suggests dislocations such as the current presentation , are extremely rare and although have been described to occur with minor trauma , have never been described as occurring following a largely atraumatic event . in such cases ,  harrowing closed reduction may be impossible even with adequate analgesia due to patellas position and soft tissues obstruction . on reviewing the previous cases of extra -articular dislocation in the literature , none had described a completely at

backgroundgraphene oxide ( go ) composites were synthesized by a microwave - assisted hydrothermal reaction . the morphologies of the products were characterized by field - emission scanning electron microscopy and tem.methodsgraphen oxide composites with different amounts of rgo were also synthesized , which include cu2o/0.25 % rgo , cu2-o/1 % rg , and cu2/1% rgo . after stirring for 2 h , the homogeneous solution was heated with stirring in the microwave system at 150 c for 3 h . after the product was cooled to rt , the final product was collected by centrifugation , washed with water five times , and dried at 70 c.resultsthe morphologies and morphological characteristics of the composites and the morphology of the reactants were characterized . the results showed that the composite composites include cu(no3)2 and cu(1)1 % .

background : median clefts are rare , disfiguring facial anomalies , with an incidence of about 1.4 to 4.9 cases per 100,000 live births.aims:the purpose of this paper is to report a case of a five - year - old boy with a facial cleft of the upper lip . the above - mentioned studies prove the versatility of the pfeifer incision.materials and methods : we report using a pfeif incision to correct a moderate true median cleft . the postoperative results were excellent and proper approximation of the orbicularis oris muscle and the vermilion was achieved with a symmetrical cupid 's bow and philtrum.results:this case was successfully treated with a complete reconstruction of the cupid and philtraum for true and false clefts , and complex craniofacial procedures in case of false median

objectiveto develop a simple , fast , and laboratory - free risk score to identify people with undiagnosed diabetes and incident diabetes in peru , a latin american country that spans coastal , andean , and rainforest settings.research design and methodsthe peruvian diabetes risk score was developed using a national population - based survey , including urban and rural areas across major geographical regions , as well as a cohort study . the data were analyzed using a cross - sectional , multistage , and independent longitudinal cohort study.resultsof the study , a total of 4,206 participants were enrolled in the eninbsc survey . the peruvian risk score ( p = 0.93 ) was used to develop the risk score , and the cohort study ( p < 0.001 ) used the hosmer - lemeshow goodness - of - fit test to assess the performance of the risk scoring.conclusionthe peruvian data suggest that the peru diabetes risk scores can be used in a pragmatic manner to identify undiosed diabetes in a wider scale and to facilitate early prevention and management strategies .

background and aims : the aim of the present study was to investigate the potential use of p53 rna interference to block p53 expression , as well as the subsequent effect on cell invasion and gene expression on the human cervical cancer cell.materials and methods : the p53sirna ( sirna interference ) treatment of 510 hela cells was performed by reverse - transfection , based on a preliminary optimised protocol for p53 sirna , using siport neofx . the invasion assays were performed on the hela cell line to evaluate the impact of the treatment on the cell viability.results:the total rna for the cells treated with 50 nm p53rna andthe control group at 24 hours post treatment was extracted using the taqman qrt - pcr assay . the total expression of vegf protein was found dowregulated in the culture medium than in the control group.conclusion:the p53 inhibition of hela expression is a natural mechanism able to specific inhibit a target gene . p53 inhibitions become dependent and rely on angiogenesis for growth and metastasis .

background : chlamydophila pneumoniae ( c. pneumoniae ) is an obligatory intracellular organism , and is responsible for at least 10% of community - acquired pneumonias . the possibility of infection by this bacterium has been suggested in the pathogenesis of atherosclerosis . the aim of this study was to determine whether there is any difference between two groups of patients with coronary atherosclerotic plaque and subjects without coronary atheroclerosis for the presence of c. pnuemoniae dna by polymerase chain reaction method.materials and methods : this case - control study was carried out on 60 coronary artery biopsies in mashhad , iran in 2010 . the dna was extracted by using proteinase k and non - heating dna extraction method.results:the mean bmi of the patients in the study and the control groups were 24.4 ( 3.77 ) and 23.9 ( 2.62 ) kg / m2 respectively . twenty - five patients were males and five patients ( 16.7% ) were females . the male to female ratio was 5:1 and no significant difference was seen between study and control groups for gender ( p = 1.00).conclusion:we did not find any significant

background : the aim of this study was to identify the spiritual health of patients with defects caused by diabetes.materials and methods : this was a qualitative - phenomenological and descriptive study and the participants were selected from rehabilitation centers in isfahan and valiasr hospital in zanjan.results:the outcome of this phase of the study was 173 codes and 2 groups that included hindering factors in spiritual health and the promotion of the relation with god.conclusion:the concepts that patients had experienced as hindering factor of the treatment process were disappointment and hopelessness , guilt , feeling distant from god , quitting obligatory acts and knowing god as cruel .

introduction : in vitro fertilization ( ivf ) has been recognized as a public health issue worldwide . the aim of this article is to present a new method of assessing the viability of euploid embryos in the culture medium . methods : we developed a method of analyzing the morphological features of the embryos , using time - lapse microscopy , and using a receiver operating characteristic ( roc ) analysis . results : the analysis of the embryonic secretome revealed that a fairly high proportion of embryos between 10 - 15% appeared to be viable.conclusion:the optimized criteria system ( ocs )  highlights 3 modified or new parameters : fragmentation ( with a more permissive criterion of < 15% in the  good  category ) ; symmetry and the blastomere number.conclusions:the use of roc analysis is related in a direct and natural way to cost / benefit analysis of diagnostic decision - making  . the roc curve of morphological versus metabolomic approach in relation to the correct prediction of pregnancy outcome .

objective . to examine the association between serum 25(oh)d3 concentrations and risk of type 2 diabetes in elderly faroese.methods . we examined 668 septuagenarians aged 7074 years ( mean age 72.5 years ) . serum hdl and triacylglycerides were measured by liquid chromatography - tandem mass spectrometry ( lc - ms / ms ) . results . the sex - adjusted odds of having diabetes ( newly diagnosed and previously diagnosed subjects ) and sufficient vitamin d status ( adjusted odds ratio [ or ] 1.80 [ 95% ci 1.232.64 ] ; p = 0.002 ) were significantly associated with the risk of newly diagnosed diabetes compared with the adjusted or for pre - existing diabetes ( or 1.48 [ 95 % ci 0.232 .64 ] ) . conclusion . vitamin d supplementation could be considered a possible means for supporting glycemic control of type two diabetes .

background : leaf senescence is the last phase of plant development and a highly coordinated process regulated by a large number of genes ( sags ) . the aim of this study was to identify putative putative sags from the arabidopsis thaliana , lycopersicon esculentum and nicotiana tabaccum.methods:we have developed a leaf senesescence related database ( lsd ) by retrieving and integrating information from research papers and public databases.results:we identified 1145 sags and mutants in 21 species . we also identified 154 putative saps from 21 plants . the information of 154 of these sags was extracted from the lsd . we have integrated the sequence similarity search tool blast and the sequence analysis platform weblab into the database.conclusion:lsd could be a useful resource and a good starting point for the further study of the molecular aspects of leaf senescentence .

background : bone mineral accrual is altered in young women with type 1 diabetes . the purpose of this study was to evaluate the effect of insulin therapy on bone mineral accumulation ( bmd ) at the femoral neck and total hip.methods:in this cross - sectional study , 63 case subjects and 85 control subjects were enrolled in the baseline study ( age < 20 years of age ) and 2 years later ( age > 20 years).results:the baseline study enrolled 72 case subjects ( diabetes duration > 2 years ) and 91 control subjects ( age = 20 years ) at ages 1539 years at enrollment.conclusion:the older cohort had significantly lower bmd at the total hip and femoral necks than control subjects . however , the percent change in bmd from baseline to follow - up was not statistically different between case and control subjects at any of the sites measured.conclusions:the results demonstrate that the older cohort has significantly lower total hip bmd than the control subjects in the 2-year interval .

sensitive skin syndrome ( sss ) is a common and challenging condition , yet little is known about its underlying pathophysiology .  sss is characterized by subjective complaints that are out of proportion to the objective clinical findings . the clinical approach to the sss demands a thorough process of eliminating obvious as well as more subtle diagnoses .  shaka specific algorithms have been developed for the management of sss but they are the result of accumulated clinical experience and lack experimental evidence to support their use . nonetheless , algorithmic thinking may aid in ensuring complete assessment .  he also suggests that the use of objective reactivity testing should be considered when considering the differential diagnosis .

backgroundspontaneous pneumothorax ( sp ) is a relatively common condition . it is classified into primary spontaneous ( psp ) and secondary spontaneous ( bpsp ) depending on the extent of the air in the pleura space . the diagnosis of bps is usually delayed and the patient is referred to a tertiary center for an elective video - assisted thoracoscopic surgery intervention ( vats).case reporta 20-year - old , overweight , greek male patient was presented to the emergency department complaining of low - intensity chest pain and breathlessness of progressive worsening over the preceding 7 days . on admission ,  the patient was managed with bilateral tube thoracostomy through the fifth intercostal spaces . during hospitalization a chest computed tomography ( ct ) was performed and revealed the presence of blebs at the apices of both upper lungs . during the next 3 days the patient remained under observation ; serial physical examinations and chest x - rays were normal . during this period the patient underwent a bilateral vats procedure . the patient remains healthy and the vats treatment was successfully managed . conclusionbpsp is a rare clinical condition and usually develops in patients with low bmi and bilateral blebs / bullae .

background and aims : familial adenomatous polyposis is one of the most common colorectal cancers , accounting for 1% of all coloresctal malignancies . the present study aims to investigate fatty acid composition in feces and to compare the effects of a cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitor on the composition of fatty acids in duodenal biopsies.materials and methods : the study included patients with ileal - pouch - anal anastomosis and ileostomy , 1870 years of age , and documented duodenum and jejunum lesions graded as spigelman i , ii , or iii and the largest adenoma10  mm.results:we found no significant relationships between the content of fecal fatty acids and dietary intake in feces , except for the estimates of the contents of delta-9-desaturase ( stearoyl - coa desaturase , scd ) activity , namely the ratio of palmitolic acid / palmitic acid in feces.conclusions:this study suggests that fatty acid content in feces may be related to dietary intake , serum lipids , and fatty acids composition in duods .

background : the purpose of this study was to compare pain levels at needle insertion , delivery of local anesthetic and tooth preparation for the computer - controlled local anesthesia ( wand ) and during tooth preparation with the wand.methods:this was a cross - sectional study of sixteen adult patients who were to undergo fixed prosthodontic treatment .  sixteen patients were randomly assigned to the wand or conventional buccal anesthesia .  the wand was used on one side for central incisors , lateral incisor , upper canine , first and second premolars , and the traditional plastic aspirating syringe injection was applied on the mucobuccal fold of the related tooth on the opposite side . results:the pain scores were significantly lower for the wand than for the conventional technique ( z = - 4.064 , p<0.01 ) . the wand technique had a lower pain level compared to the conventional syringe ( z= - 3.897 , p>0.05 ) . during the anesthetic solution delivery phase , the pain level for wand technique was again highly significantly lower than for conventional technique.conclusion:the wand anesthesia technique appears advantageous for restorative procedures because more teeth are anesthetized with one palatal injection , and

backgroundchronic obstructive pulmonary disease ( copd ) is the fourth most common cause of death in the world and is predicted by the world health organisation to be a major health concern in the coming decade . the aim of this study was to evaluate the feasibility of remote pulse oximetry in a remote exercise session.methods and resultsa total of thirty - seven healthy individuals ( 14 males and 23 females ) completed a single remote cardiopulmonary exercise session using the ehab telerehabilitation system ( v2.0 , uniquest , brisbane , australia ) . the participant 's heart rate ( hr ) and pulse oximeter ( spo2 ) were recorded in real time using a 100  mm visual analogue scale ( vas ) . data was stored in a database stored locally on the computer .  the data was analysed using statistical package for the social sciences ( v17.0 spss inc . , chicago , il ) . a p value of < 0.05 was considered to be significant.resultsthe mean ( sd ) transmission latency was 2.94 ( 0.59 ) seconds.conclusionthe ehab device was safe and easy to use .

background : despite 15 years of the global  safe motherhood   approach , maternal and child health outcomes remain a high priority in resource - poor countries . this report highlights examples of national programs and projects indicating that such approaches are feasible and acceptable to the population.methods:the first phase of implementation of preconception care in a northern county ( mancheng , in hebei province ) during february and march , 2004 was a pilot social marketing effort . the first step was to develop a test plan to promote preconception health care . the second phase was to disseminate information about the benefits of preconceptions care to the general public and health care providers . the third stage was to evaluate the feasibility of implementing the program in a rural area . the final stage was the evaluation of the effectiveness of the test plan.results:during the first quarter of 2002 , 91% of 10,000 questionnaires were completed , and 140 subjects were interviewed , including women of reproductive age , maternal / child health workers , and local government officials.conclusion:despite the low fertility rates and high rates of birth defects ( 2 to 3% of all live births ) in south korea , preconception services are feasible .

Scythe was the only patient in the study group with  Scythe levels above 100  pg / ml . ahime and  Scythe were the other two groups with  weap levels above 200  pg/ ml .

key clinical messagethe patellar periprosthetic fracture after total knee arthroplasty is a very uncommon entity , and challenging to treat , especially the open ones , because of the high risk of infectious complications . in this article , we report a unique case of an open patella fracture above a total knee knee arroplasty , sustained by a 56-year - old female patient .

background : near - infrared ( nir ) radiation can penetrate the skin and the sclera of the eye . however , in the absence of suitable protection , nir radiation can affect tissues deeper within the eye , such as muscles , the lens , and retina . the human cornea plays a critical role in refracting light onto the retina and also protects the eye against external agents . the purpose of this study was to investigate changes in genetic expression in human corneal epithelial tissue after nir irradiation to simulate solar nir exposure that reaches human tissues.methods:the three - dimensional reconstructed human epithelial model ( labcyte cornea - model ) prepared from enzymatically digested normal human cells was compared with the control group . dna microarray and real - time polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) analysis were performed to determine the upregulated and downregulated genes in the nir - irradiated group . the pcr analysis was performed using the dna engine opticon 2 system ( bio - rad laboratories inc . ) . all six whole individual three -dimensional reconstructed human corna samples were analyzed using the genespring gx 11.0 software ( agilent technologies).results:all statistical data were presented as the mean

background : hypertensive crisis is a common chronic medical condition affecting people in pakistan and the rest of the world . it is an important modifiable risk factor for cardiovascular morbidity and mortality , particularly for stroke ( accounting for 51% of all stroke deaths worldwide ) , ischemic heart disease , chronic kidney disease , congestive heart failure , aortic aneurysm , and peripheral arterial disease.objectives:the aim of this study was to determine the prevalence of hypertension , its management , and its outcome in patients presenting to a tertiary care center in karachi.patients and methods : a total of 73,063 hypertensive patients presented to the aga khan university hospital between years 2005 and 2010 . the prevalence ( % ) of uncontrolled hypertension was 28.9% ( 387 ) . the mean length of stay was longer in patients with hypertension than in those with uncontrolled hypertension . the systolic blood pressure ( sbp ) and diastolic blood pressures ( dbp ) were recorded from vital sheets for nursing services.results:the prevalence of hypertensive crises was 47.7% ( 104 ) in patients who were known hypertensive and had uncontrolled hypertension.conclusion:despite the high prevalence , hypertension can progress to hypert

the p2y12 receptor subtype   belonging to the gi class of g protein - coupled receptors is activated by adp . the expression of this receptor is variable with postnatal development and shows sexually dimorphic behavior . through the use of all the available antibodies recognizing the c - terminus or the second intracellular loop of the receptor , the aim of the present work is to provide comparative evidence about the expression and function of p2-y12-immunoreactive antibodies in microglia versus oligodendrocytes in neuroinflammatory conditions such as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis ( als ) and multiple sclerosis ( ms ) .  we show that p2_12 receptor is abundantly and strongly immunoreactive with the microglias population and colocalizing with the majority of cd11b - positive cells , in both dorsal ( dh ) and ventral ( vh ) horns of spinal cord sections .  furthermore , we observe a predominance of c - ter immunoreactivity ( red ) compared to that of mhc ii ( green ) , as well as a major occurrence of red signal .  these results confirm that the p2 -y12 protein is abundantly expressed in both primary and secondary microglial precursor cells

backgroundacute kidney injury ( aki ) requiring dialysis is a serious complication in critically ill patients , bringing increased morbidity , mortality , and costs of care . we hypothesized that the degree of severity of kidney injury prior to dialysis may discriminate risk - adjusted mortality risk in patients with aki.methods and resultsa total of 2,744 patients ( 71,090/324,999 ) who required new dialysis ( stage iii aki according to the akin classification system ) were included in the study .  the study accounts for differences in patient characteristics using a previously validated logistic regression model that predicts mortality risk for each individual patient.resultsaki - d patients were classified as stage iii ( or severe ) aki . the median time was 43.0 , 39.5 , and 67.4 hours in groups i , ii , and iii , whereas patients in other groups required dialysis with little or no elevation in serum creatinine were more likely to require dialysis.conclusionthe study confirms prior multicenter observations that aki requiring dials in icu usually represents a group of patients that sustain acute kidney injury on an underlying renal dysfunction .  furthermore , the results indicate that majority of aki - patients sustain mild - to - moderate

background : the current psychiatric services in japan are not adequate to meet the complex mental health needs of people with i d and other developmental disabilities appropriately , sensitively , and effectively . the paucity of trained psychiatrists and other allied professionals is a major barrier to the development of this subspecialty in many countries.aims:the purpose of this study was to obtain a clear perspective on the mental health and distress of parents having children with developmental disabilities.materials and methods : this facility - based nationwide survey included 1,102 institutions and consultation centers in all prefectures of japan , which were listed in the national register of agencies , organizations , and institutions related to intellectual disabilities   and the directory of support centers for individuals with developmental disorders .  the questionnaire was mailed in october 2008 , with an explanatory letter to the person in charge of each institution or consultation center . when agreement was met to participate in this study , one or more service providers in each facility completed the questionnaire based on all sources of information available . the information disclosed in this questionnaire was divided into two sections with a total of 47 multiple choice questions . the first section sought information about staff adequacy , the scale of the facility , the health promotion services provided , the ages of their clients ,

in 2001 , 24 cases of s. typhimurium dt160 salmonellosis were reported in the auckland region compared with an average of four sporadic cases each month with this serotype in the previous 7 months . s. chorizoides , a common foodborne pathogen , was identified as the source of the outbreak . s . chorozides , s. enterica , and s. japonica were also identified .

non - crystallographic symmetry ( ncs ) is common in macromolecular crystals , occurring in about 1/3 or more of structures in the protein data bank . though non - crystallography increases the complexity of structure determination , in most cases it yields a distinct advantage in this process because it brings with it information on relationships between density in different parts of the crystal and between coordinates in different regions of the structure . the correlation of ncs - related density is examined in the original electron density map , and the ncs operators are refined to maximize the correlation among all copies of the density in the map .  the results show that this approach correctly identified all 5 regions of density that had high similarity to the test density .

backgroundthe aim of this study was to identify specific behavioral risk factors for overweight in young children and to assess their quantitative contribution to the prevalence of overweight in the general population and in high risk sub groups.methodswe prospectively investigated associations between dietary habits , screen time and overweight when the children were 5 and 7 years old and overweight at the age of 8 years.resultsthe association between screen time , overweight and obesity was strongest in children of mothers with low education ( 27.5% versus 19.4% ) and in children with no breast feeding ( 19.7% versus 15.9% ) . the association between overweight and screen time weakened significantly in the model including the behavioral factors ( 5% change in the regression coefficient from 1.07 to 1.02 ) . in addition , the associations with screen time were weaker in the models including behavioral risk factor and overweight . the associations between screen times and overweight weakened only marginally in the analysis in the imputed data . the effects of these factors on the children 's weight gain were not statistically significant in the analyses in the pooled data sets .

background : the proliferation of primary tumor lesions causes the detachment of tumor cells , which penetrate vessel walls to enter the general lymph or blood circulation system for the invasion of distal tissues , where tumor  seeds can be found . this study aimed to describe the mechanism of p15 gene mutation in the occurrence and metastasis of osteosarcoma in a rat model , as previously described.methods:a total of 60 sd rats were randomly divided into control and model groups ( n=30 each ) .  shaper rats were inoculated with a total of 25 peripheral blood samples in the osteosarccoma model group . the tumor size and pulmonary tissue damage , along with the measurement of tumor size , were measured.results:during 0 , 1 , 2 , 4 , and 6 weeks of the inoculation , 18 of them ( 72% ) showed mutation in 2 exon of p14 gene , while control rats had normal gene isoforms .  Scythe on the first week , most of the tumor lesions underneath the skin around the injection sites were detected . after 26 weeks , abnormal tumor - like structures began to occur in the pulmonary tissues in the model group along with elevated tumor - positive cell percentage in primary and pulmonary tissues , as consistent with previous reports.con

background : trichoscopy is a noninvasive and in vivo diagnostic technique for differential diagnosis of various hair and scalp diseases . the authors believe that trichoscopic patterns are specific to trichotillomania and may aide in early diagnosis of this chronic condition.methods:this study was carried out on 10 patients attending department of dermatology in a tertiary hospital attached to s. nijalingappa medical college at bagalkot , south india between january 2014 and july 2014.results:a total of 10 patients with clinical features of ttm were subjected for a complete history and dermatological examination .  the most common trichoscope pattern observed in all the patients was decreased hair density and hairs broken at different lengths ( 100% ) . the most commonly observed patterns were trichoptilosis ( yellow arrows ) , tulip hairs ( red arrows ) and perifollicular whitish areas ( red stars ) . all of them presented with patchy loss of hair over the scalp .

rectal syphilis is a rare , sexually transmitted disease . it is usually asymptomatic and less frequently presents as proctitis , ulcer and pseudotumors . in western countries the incidence of syphilis has risen , mainly in the 20 - 24 age group and its clinical manifestations have been various . in korea , it is difficult to diagnose and physicians occasionally might prescribe inappropriate treatments . in this paper  Scythe we describe a case of primary rectal venous syphilis which was suspected to be rectal cancer .  Scythe was treated with intramuscular penicillin g benzathine .

background : vagus nerve stimulation during ischemia / reperfusion ( i / r ) has been shown to have protective effects on gastric injury and peptic ulcer.objectives:the present study was performed to investigate the protective effect of interaction between melatonin ( melatonin ) and vagus ( vagotomy ) in gastric tissue.patients and methods : the experimental groups were : 1 ) base+i / r+vehicle ; 2 ) base +i /r+melatonin ; 3 ) vagotomy+i/r+ vehicle ; 4 ) vagus stimulation+i - r+mel

backgroundthe chinese famine affected the entire mainland of china , but the severity varied across regions due to different weather conditions , population density , and local policies regarding food shortage . to explore whether the associations between fetal exposure to severe famine and hyperglycemia were affected by an improved nutritional environment in later life , we stratified the analyses by dietary patterns , economic status , and bmi in adulthood.methodswe conducted a multistage survey of 1,874 chinese adults born between october 1 , 1958 and september 30 , 1964 . lucent subjects were classified into five exposure cohorts : nonexposed cohort , fetal - exposed cohort , early childhood cohort , mid childhood cohort and late childhood cohort.resultsin the nonexposed group , the odds ratio of hyperglycemic exposure was higher in subjects exposed to the severe chinese famine during fetal life than in those who consumed an affluent / western diet.conclusionswe found a significant association between severe famine exposure during fetal years and an increased risk of hypercemic hyperglycymia in adulthood .

key clinical messagethe genome wide case - control association studies ( gwass ) have identified several susceptibility loci with small effects on type 2 diabetes ( t2d ) risk , but few of the associated snps , identified primarily in european ancestry populations , are replicated in african american ( aa ) populations . we report linkage and association analysis using genotypes on 9,203 fine - mapping snps added to five chromosomal regions confirmed each linkage and identified potential susceptibility genes in each of the regions .

the circadian system is a supraphysiological system that regulates biological functions in 24 h , such as sleep / activity , temperature , heart rate , glucose level , cortisol production , and oxidative stress . in mammals ,  the circadian system has a central clock localized in the suprachiasmatic nucleus of the hypothalamus ( scn ) and in a series of peripheral oscillators such as the liver , lung , adrenal gland , fibroblast cells , and others tissues . at the molecular level , the clock genes are involved in regulating biological functions such as metabolism , angiogenesis , and cellular proliferation . in addition , the genetic components of clock genes have been detected to modulate the risk of developing various diseases . in this review , we will focus on the hypothesis which states that the variation of genetic components , similar to environmental changes , can affect several physiological systems and produce an elevation of the risk for developing a disease .

the ubiquitously expressed protein phosphatase 2a ( pp2a ) has been proposed as a potential therapeutic target for the treatment of a number of pathologies ranging from neurodegenerative diseases such as alzheimer disease to neoplasias .  the b family of regulatory subunits is composed of a catalytic subunit ( c ) a scaffold subunit and one member of four families of regulatory subsunits ( b ) . the b - regulatory subunit is incorporated into the holoenzyme complex and is required to recruit and dephosphorylate target substrates . recent structural studies of the b subunit have revealed that the b- regulatory subsunit is required for the incorporation of the c subunit into the pp2 a holoenzymes . this mechanism of substrate specificity is highlighted by extensive studies of b family subunits .  this review will focus on our emerging understanding of the pp 2a substrate specificity , which appears to involve additive effects of multiple discrete interactions .

this paper presents a model for the evolution of small - world networks in schizophrenia . we show that , in the absence of a critical threshold , a self - organizing network can not sustain its local clustering , well before global connectivity breaks down . we demonstrate that , by combining adaptive rewiring with hebbian adaptive structural self - organization , the network can emerge from an initially random configuration , with minimal connectivity close to random network percolation . we propose that this is a critical breakdown in the ability of the brain to establish and maintain a modular small- world structure .

dysphagia aortica is the subjective awareness of difficulty in the passage of solids or liquids from the oropharynx to the stomach . dysphagia can be classified into an orophropharyngeal or an esophageal location , and it is caused by neuromuscular motility disorders and mechanical obstruction . mechanical dysphagias is associated with intrinsic or extrinsic compression , resulting in progressive intolerance to solids . the term , dysphagiae aortic aorta , has been used to describe difficulty in swallowing caused by external compression from an ectatic , tortuous , or aneurysmal aortas as a result of age - related degeneration . we report herein a patient with dysphagiosis associated with an aortacal aneurism .

flibanserin is an agent of 5-hydroxytryptamin ( 5-ht ) type 1a receptor and an antagonist of the 5-hex type 2a and thus a new molecular entity . in clinical stage 2a as antidepressant drug , flibanerin could not prove its effectiveness , but almost no sexual function disorder was reported in subjects . for this reason , the arizona sexual experiences scale ( asex ) was used for making a comparison of the sexual function effectiveness of flibansingerin on antidepressant vs. placebo proven in stage 2b studies among four depression programs . flibinserin showed a statistically significant increase in satisfying sexual events ( sse ) and female sexual function index ( fsfi ) and reduction in female sexual distress scale - revised ( fsds - r ) compared to placebo . in the asex , the adverse effects most common were vertigo , fatigue , vomiting , insomnia , and mouth dryness .

key clinical messagethe keratoacanthoma ( kcm ) is a rapidly evolving tumor , composed of keratinizing squamous cells originating in pilosebaceous follicles and resolving spontaneously if left untreated . it usually occurs over extremities and scalp is unusual site for development of lesion .  we report a rare case of kcm occurring over scalp which is successfully treated with surgical excision .
